---
title: "Drafting Space"
format: html
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a
finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Pseudo Code

Lexer Character by character

For after each normal character, If a special character or space, then
the running "read" is a token, else keep reading

After a special character, if it was a paren or dot that's it, else if a
lambda then continue reading for the full token.

\*only accept a not-null, not-empty expression

PrimeNewTokenRead: runningRead = fullExpression\[1\], fullExpression =
fullExpression\[2:\] #all except the first character

repeat until processed:

if runningRead\[-1\] %in% c("(",")",".") \| (runningRead\[-1\] is a char
& fullExpression\[1\] is not a char)

then add runningRead to tokens, if fullExpression!="", PrimeNewTokenRead

elseif fullExpression\[1\] is a char

then ReadLetter

else throw error, may be trying to bind a syntax symbol.

console print the reading state for tracking

ReadLetter:

if fullExpression not "",

then runningRead+=fullExpression\[1\],

fullExpression\[2:\]

|      |           |           |           |             |             |
|------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
|      | (         | )         | .         | \\          | char        |
| (    | set token | set token | set token | throw error | set token   |
| )    | set token | set token | set token | throw error | set token   |
| .    | set token | set token | set token | throw error | set token   |
| \\   | set token | set token | set token | throw error | set token   |
| char | set token | set token | set token | add to read | add to read |

V2.0

Not allowed: a symbol or space after lambda, binding a symbol or space,
or a parenthesis pair around a binding (detected via closing paren.
after a period)

\\ then non-char: Syntax error "Binding an invalid term after lambda"

non-char then . : Syntax error "Binding an invalid term before period"

. then ) : Syntax error : Syntax error "Invalid parenthesis around
binding term"

char or lambda then char : Read

else: set token

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|----|----|----|----|----|----|----|
| 2ndV 1st\> | ( | ) | . | \\ | " " | char |
| ( | set token | set token | set token | Syntax error | set token | set token |
| ) | Syntax error | set token | Syntax error | Syntax error | set token | set token |
| . | Syntax error | Syntax error | Syntax error | Syntax error | Syntax error | set token |
| \\ | set token | set token | set token | Syntax error | set token | set token |
| " " | set token | set token | set token | Syntax error | set token | set token |
| char | set token | set token | set token | Read | set token | Read |

```{r}
#install.packages("foreach")
library(foreach)

#foreach(i=1:length(fullExpression)) %do% {
#  ...
#}

#print(paste("Hello", name)) is the syntax for printing


if(interactive()) 
  {fullExpression <- readline(prompt="Enter your expression: ")}
#test

  # TRUE if non-symbol (letter/digit/etc.), FALSE for ()./\
IsChar <- function(ch) {
  nchar(ch) > 0 && !ch %in% c("(", ")", ".", "/", "\\", " ")
} #nchar(ch) > 0 makes sure the argument isn't empty

# Sets the first char of fullExpression to runningRead, taking first char
PrimeNewTokenRead <- function() {
  runningRead    <<- substr(fullExpression, 1, 1)
  fullExpression <<- substr(fullExpression, 2, nchar(fullExpression))
}

# Moves the first char of fullExpression to runningRead
ReadLetter <- function() {
  if (fullExpression != "") {
    runningRead    <<- paste0(runningRead, substr(fullExpression, 1, 1))
    fullExpression <<- substr(fullExpression, 2, nchar(fullExpression))
  }
}

# ── Main tokenising loop ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

tokens <- c()
PrimeNewTokenRead()

processed <- FALSE
while (!processed) {

  # Peek at boundary characters
  lastChar  <- substr(runningRead,    nchar(runningRead),    nchar(runningRead))
  firstChar <- substr(fullExpression, 1, 1)   # "" when fullExpression is empty

  cat(sprintf(
    "[Reading] runningRead: %-12s | firstChar: %-4s | tokens so far: [%s]\n",
    paste0("'", runningRead, "'"),
    paste0("'", firstChar,   "'"),
    paste(tokens, collapse = ", ")
  )) #paste0 and sprintf are used to create strings but not print them, then cat concatenates and prints to the console.

  if (lastChar %in% c("(", ")", ".", " ") ||
      (IsChar(lastChar) && !IsChar(firstChar))) {
    # ── Token boundary reached: flush runningRead ──
    tokens <- c(tokens, runningRead)
    if (fullExpression != "") {
      PrimeNewTokenRead()
    } else {
      processed <- TRUE
    }

  } else if (IsChar(firstChar)) {
    # ── Still inside a multi-char token: keep reading ──
    ReadLetter()

  } else {
    stop("Parse error: may be trying to bind a syntax symbol ('",
         firstChar, "').")
  }
}

cat("\nFinal tokens:", paste(tokens, collapse = " | "), "\n")

```

Parser Token by token ( makes new branch ) ends and returns a level
\*should handle main dependency-levelling, just needs left and right
association for application and abstraction, respectively. I don't need
the tree to be binary or efficient hypothetically, so long as evaluation
can be done step by step using pairs.

/ denotes a binding token, drop / to read name . denotes a separation,
shouldn't be needed, drop . to process semantics, term after binding is
bound so no symbol needed.

Binding token and term token together should be the abstraction to apply
to another term.

```{r}

```

Tree visualizing library in R?

(X(y)) ((X)y)

```{r}
#| echo: false
#The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
```
